question: Electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) is the treatment of choice in:
Which of the following is the most common indication for Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT)?
Which of the following is considered an emergency indication for ECT?
Which of the following psychiatric disorders may respond well to ECT?
Which of the following is TRUE regarding ECT?
options: It is administered under general anesthesia with muscle relaxation.
It causes permanent memory loss in all patients.
It is contraindicated in all elderly patients.

question: Which of the following is the most common adverse effect of ECT?
Which of the following patients is the best candidate for ECT?
options: A patient with severe depression who has not responded to medications.

question: Which of the following statements regarding ECT is FALSE?
options: It can be life-saving in severe depression.
It may be indicated in catatonia.
It is considered the first-line treatment for Generalized Anxiety Disorder.
It is effective in some severe mood disorders.

question: Which of the following is an important pre-ECT assessment?
options: Medical evaluation and fitness for anesthesia.

question: Which of the following statements regarding Electroconvulsive Therapy is TRUE?
options: ECT is an effective treatment for selected severe psychiatric disorders, particularly severe depression and catatonia.
ECT has no role in psychiatry.
ECT is used only after every psychotropic medication has failed.
ECT causes irreversible cognitive impairment in all patients.
